title: Why is Evangelion Vox so derided
display_title: Why is Evangelion Vox so derided
wikitext:
[]: Why is Evangelion Vox so derided?             Quote Post by chaosakita » Mon Aug 08, 2011 6:51 pm For the people who aren't familiar with it, Evnagelion Vox is an arrange album of various tracks from Evangelion made by the same people who arranged Thanatos (If I Can't Be Yours). It seems like everyone hates this album and thinks that it has no redeeming value. But I recently downloaded it, and I find it's pretty interesting. Loren is a very nice singer, and while the tracks can be kind of silly, it find it ok to listen to. In particular, I really enjoy the song "Star". It'd be track I'd use as the credits music for EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Mon Aug 08, 2011 7:23 pm Yeah, I love EVA VOX too! In fact, I listen to it every now ane again if I wanna here something EVA related. I think the reason people hate it is because the EVA fanbase is composed of people who don't have a taste for pop culture, of which the Rap/R&B genre of music falls under. That or they just haven't grown up on the stuff and don't have that inate "black rhythm soul" that I enjoy from it.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Aug 08, 2011 8:41 pm Because Evangelion and rap is the worst idea ever. Worse than Asuka X Touji in The End of Evangelion alternate live action.

[]: Re: Why is Evangelion Vox so derided?             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Aug 08, 2011 9:01 pm Because haters are always more noisy than lovers; I really love this album but I simply never mentioned it recently, I especially like "Armageddon" (since I've heard the "Symphony of Evangelion" concert) and "Utopia". The Eva Monkey wrote: Worse than Asuka X Touji in The End of Evangelion alternate live action.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Mon Aug 08, 2011 9:26 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Because Evangelion and rap is the worst idea ever. Worse than Asuka X Touji in The End of Evangelion alternate live action.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Aug 09, 2011 10:04 am chaosakita wrote: But most of it isn't rap. (I typed in "rape" first though...)

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Aug 09, 2011 10:20 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Aug 09, 2011 1:49 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: The rap tracks are just not very good. The rest of the album is a lot of awesome fun. I wrote rather extensively about it in case anyone wants to read it.

[]:             Quote Post by Born of Lilith » Thu Aug 25, 2011 2:58 pm Add me to the devil's advocate heap here. The trip hop tracks are gorgeous, especially "The Image of Me"; I'd even say that that one almost rivals "Thanatos (If I Can't Be Yours)" in its inspired interpretation of a piece from the Eva soundtrack. The Eva Monkey wrote: Because Evangelion and rap is the worst idea ever.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Thu Aug 25, 2011 4:25 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Because Evangelion and rap is the worst idea ever.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Thu Aug 25, 2011 5:12 pm I’m quite fond of it myself. In fact, since I bothered to download it and all, I should probably listen to it more… (I know what I’m going to do right now!)

[]:             Quote Post by Crazy Packers Fan » Wed Aug 31, 2011 2:33 am The LOREN version of "Komm, susser Tod" is quite good. And I like how it has that Thanatos interlude that's part of "If I Can't Be Yours." Who knows what the actual lyrics are, it sounds to me like "I'm so pity, a pity a pity, a pity."

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sun Oct 23, 2011 7:56 pm Crazy Packers Fan wrote: The LOREN version of "Komm, susser Tod" is quite good. And I like how it has that Thanatos interlude that's part of "If I Can't Be Yours." Who knows what the actual lyrics are, it sounds to me like "I'm so pity, a pity a pity, a pity."

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Thu Oct 27, 2011 1:40 pm I liked "image of me" The rest of it was meh. I actually hated Armageddon thought it was bleh. All in all though it wasn't too bad though. @shin-chan: I wanna see a 10 hours of Shinji squishing kaworu with unit 1 video.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Sun Oct 30, 2011 6:58 pm Crazy Packers Fan wrote: The LOREN version of "Komm, susser Tod" is quite good. And I like how it has that Thanatos interlude that's part of "If I Can't Be Yours." Who knows what the actual lyrics are, it sounds to me like "I'm so pity, a pity a pity, a pity."

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Oct 30, 2011 7:14 pm Sephizim wrote: I hear "Ah, so, beer yay, beer yay, oh beer yay, oh beer yay." Perhaps its a chorus of Misatos?

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Sun Oct 30, 2011 7:19 pm Does anyone know the story behind this album? Gainax just fire off commish to any reggae-rap label with an office in Tokyo?

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Oct 30, 2011 7:32 pm arkiel wrote: Does anyone know the story behind this album? Gainax just fire off commish to any reggae-rap label with an office in Tokyo?

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Oct 30, 2011 8:42 pm They did Thanatos.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Mon Oct 31, 2011 8:58 am TehDonutKing wrote: They did Thanatos.
